findings.description: The PDF presents all data in a single wide horizontal table fitting on one page, while the HTML renders it as a tall vertical table with wrapped cell content, significantly changing the layout from a compact wide format to a long narrow format.
findings.evidence: PDF shows a single-page wide table with all columns visible horizontally. HTML shows a narrower table where Owner-Facility text wraps extensively, making each row much taller and the table spans many vertical screens.

findings.description: Table column headers differ slightly in labeling but contain equivalent information. The structure (rows and columns) is preserved with the same 18 data rows and same column count.
findings.evidence: PDF header 'Owner - Facility' vs HTML 'Owner - Facility'; PDF 'As Built Flow MGD' vs HTML column split similarly. All 18 rows are present in both versions with matching data.

findings.description: The title formatting differs slightly - PDF has centered title in two lines, HTML has a left-aligned title with the subtitle as a separate section header.
findings.evidence: PDF: centered 'DWR Water Quality Programs / January 2014 Civil Penalty Assessments'. HTML: left-aligned bold 'DWR Water Quality Programs' with horizontal rule, then 'January 2014 Civil Penalty Assessments' as separate text.

findings.description: The 'Total Cases: 18' footer line is preserved in the HTML, appearing at the bottom of the table.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML show 'Total Cases: 18' at the bottom.
